name: SMS Phishing (Smishing) Response
description: Responds to SMS phishing (smishing) attacks targeting employees. Extracts indicators from the reported SMS message including phone numbers, URLs, and keywords. URLs are enriched against threat intelligence. Confirmed smishing triggers blocking of the sender phone number, URL blocking on the web proxy, notification to affected users, and a SOC alert. Handles the growing attack vector of SMS-based social engineering.
author: T1 Agentics

required_integrations.name: VirusTotal
required_integrations.reason: Enrich URLs extracted from SMS messages

data.title: Smishing Report Received
data.summary: Triggers on SMS phishing or smishing reports

data.title: Extract SMS Indicators
data.summary: Parse phone numbers, URLs, and suspicious keywords from the SMS content

script.code:
import re

def main(alert):
    body = alert.get('body', alert.get('sms_content', alert.get('message', '')))
    url_pattern = r'https?://[^\s<>"]+'
    urls = list(set(re.findall(url_pattern, body)))
    phone_pattern = r'[\+]?[1-9]\d{6,14}'
    phones = list(set(re.findall(phone_pattern, body)))
    sender_phone = alert.get('sender_phone', alert.get('from_number', ''))
    keywords = ['urgent', 'verify', 'suspend', 'click', 'confirm', 'expire', 'account', 'bank', 'password']
    found_keywords = [kw for kw in keywords if kw.lower() in body.lower()]
    return {
        'urls': urls,
        'url_count': len(urls),
        'phone_numbers': phones,
        'sender_phone': sender_phone,
        'suspicious_keywords': found_keywords,
        'keyword_count': len(found_keywords),
        'sms_body': body[:500]
    }
